title: Acritopappus micropappus
intro: Acritopappus micropappus, also known as the small-flowered acritopappus, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Acritopappus micropappus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
